def solve(*args, **kwargs):
    # Enumerate failure modes for the problem of extending GET /api/v1/problems
    # to filter by arbitrary skill tags (case-insensitive, comma-separated or array),
    # composing with difficulty and limit, preserving stable sorting.
    failure_modes = [
        {
            "id": 1,
            "name": "Incorrect Tag Filtering Logic (Case Sensitivity and Parsing Errors)",
            "rationale": (
                "The API must handle tags in a case-insensitive manner and accept multiple input formats "
                "(comma-separated strings or arrays). Failure to normalize case or parse inputs correctly "
                "can lead to incomplete or incorrect filtering results, causing the UI to display wrong problem sets."
            ),
            "tests": [
                "Test with tags as comma-separated string with mixed case",
                "Test with tags as array with mixed case",
                "Test with tags containing spaces and empty entries",
                "Test with tags not present in any problem",
            ],
        },
        {
            "id": 2,
            "name": "Improper Composition with Existing Difficulty and Limit Filters",
            "rationale": (
                "The new tag filters must compose correctly with existing difficulty and limit filters. "
                "Failure to combine filters properly can cause incorrect problem sets or incorrect counts."
            ),
            "tests": [
                "Test filtering by tags and difficulty simultaneously",
                "Test filtering by tags with limit applied",
                "Test filtering by tags with no difficulty filter",
                "Test filtering by tags with difficulty filter but no tags",
            ],
        },
        {
            "id": 3,
            "name": "Unstable Sorting After Filtering",
            "rationale": (
                "The API must preserve stable sorting of problems after applying tag filters. "
                "Failure to do so can cause UI flickering or inconsistent problem order."
            ),
            "tests": [
                "Test that sorting order is preserved after filtering by tags",
                "Test that sorting order is preserved when no tags are provided",
                "Test that sorting order is preserved with combined filters",
            ],
        },
        {
            "id": 4,
            "name": "Incorrect Metadata Payload for Normalized Tag Filters",
            "rationale": (
                "The API must surface the normalized tag filters in the metadata payload. "
                "Failure to do so can cause UI to not reflect the actual filters applied."
            ),
            "tests": [
                "Test metadata includes normalized tags after filtering",
                "Test metadata tags are lowercased and deduplicated",
                "Test metadata tags are empty when no tags filter applied",
            ],
        },
        {
            "id": 5,
            "name": "Edge Cases and Input Validation",
            "rationale": (
                "The API must handle edge cases such as empty tag filters, very large tag lists, "
                "and invalid input formats gracefully without crashing or returning incorrect results."
            ),
            "tests": [
                "Test empty tag filter input",
                "Test very large number of tags",
                "Test invalid tag input types (e.g., numbers, objects)",
                "Test tags with special characters",
            ],
        },
    ]

    # Return the failure modes list for further processing or testing
    return failure_modes
